query: SELECT id, event_date, title, summary, category, public_status, status FROM timeline_events ORDER BY event_date ASC LIMIT 30;
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2N1WTErRdSjheBh.e46440e12186 --database 3yhU3DrKzf8Z5BNtvxatFF --execute SELECT id, event_date, title, summary, category, public_status, status FROM timeline_events ORDER BY event_date ASC LIMIT 30;

rows.title: Defendant invokes speedy-trial right on record
rows.summary: SEED: First on-record demand for trial within statutory time; oral and written assertions documented.

rows.title: Counsel waives time over recorded objection
rows.summary: SEED: Continuance entered; defendant's contemporaneous written objection filed and noted in subsequent filings.

rows.title: Pro se motion to dismiss for speedy-trial violation filed
rows.summary: SEED: Pro se motion filed while counsel remained of record. Motion's disposition is among the records being publicly tracked.

rows.title: Defendant requests self-representation (Faretta)
rows.summary: SEED: On-record request to represent himself. Documentation of any subsequent canvass is being sought via public records.

rows.title: Competency raised by counsel after rights assertions
rows.summary: SEED: Competency proceedings initiated following speedy-trial and self-representation requests. Temporal proximity documented.

rows.title: Pretrial detention exceeds one year without trial
rows.summary: SEED: Custody count surpasses 365 days; no trial date set or held.

rows.title: Public records request filed with court administration
rows.summary: SEED: PRR seeking docket time-to-trial and pro se filing acceptance practices in Dept. ___ for the prior 24 months.

rows.title: Family-harm documentation collected
rows.summary: SEED: Caregiver-impact and dependent-care documentation collected during ongoing pretrial detention.

rows.title: Defendant Files Motions to Dismiss, Disqualify, and Quash
rows.summary: On December 24, 2024, Cameron Doyle Church filed multiple motions, including a motion to dismiss, a motion to disqualify the presiding judge for 'criminal misconduct' and 'judicial incompetence,' and a motion to quash.
